defects.symptom: reality-check files false 'contradicted' claims (one session: 21-22/22 = 100% false positives) and --demote then silently corrupts the index by flipping confidence to AMBIGUOUS in feature.json + INDEX.json. Three independent false-positive sources, all reproduced at HEAD in a temp repo: (a) bare-basename citations like `__init__.py:98` resolve to an arbitrary same-basename file (often an empty sibling __init__.py) instead of the feature's own file -> 'file has 0 line(s), claim cites line 98'; (b) path:line citations to real files absent from map/files.json (package.json, the feature's own spec.md) -> 'file not found' even though the file exists on disk; (c) `X` uses `Y` claims where Y is stdlib/third-party (os.environ.setdefault) -> 'symbol object not found in map/symbols.json' = contradicted. Sessions had to reword correct docs to dodge the parser. Covers evidence items 2, 3, and the false-contradiction half of item 8.
locations: /mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/memory/dummyindex/dummyindex/context/domains/reality_check.py:244-256 (file:line resolution: membership test against map/files.json only, then basename fallback `candidates[0]` over a frozenset -> arbitrary, order-nondeterministic pick; 'file not found' issued without ever checking repo_root on disk)

/mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/memory/dummyindex/dummyindex/context/domains/reality_check.py:210-219 (calls/uses: subject or object bare-name absent from symbols.json -> status 'contradicted', no external-symbol allowance)

/mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/memory/dummyindex/dummyindex/context/domains/reality_check.py:283-291 (_bare_name strips dotted prefixes, so os.environ.setdefault -> 'setdefault', destroying the only signal that the token is non-repo)

/mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/memory/dummyindex/dummyindex/context/domains/reality_check.py:464-498 (demote_feature_on_contradiction writes AMBIGUOUS into feature.json + INDEX.json off these false contradictions)
/mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/memory/dummyindex/dummyindex/cli/reality_check.py:58-59 (--demote wiring)
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED by code read + live repro (python3 -m dummyindex context reality-check --demote in a mktemp dir). (a) _verify_claim's file:line branch (reality_check.py:246-251) falls back to a basename scan of the map/files.json frozenset and takes candidates[0] - an arbitrary element with no disambiguation against the feature's own `files` list (available in feature.json via the read_feature_files idiom, dev_pick.py:325-334) and no 'ambiguous' outcome for multi-candidate basenames. (b) Resolution never consults the filesystem before declaring 'file not found' (line 250), and _load_file_paths (lines 371-385) only knows code files from map/files.json, so package.json / spec.md cites are structurally unresolvable. (c) The calls/uses branch (lines 213-219) treats absence from map/symbols.json as positive contradiction, though absence proves nothing for stdlib/third-party objects; _bare_name (lines 283-291) discards the dotted prefix that would identify them. demote_feature_on_contradiction then persists the damage. Existing test test_contradicted_call_flagged (tests/context/domains/test_reality_check.py:174) encodes the defective missing-symbol=contradicted policy.
defects.proposed_fix: Surgical changes inside reality_check.py keeping the frozen-dataclass/immutable style. (1) Thread the feature's files into verification: in reality_check_feature (line 124), load feature.json's `files` list (json read, mirroring read_feature_files) and pass `feature_files: frozenset[str]` plus `feat_dir` into _verify_claim. (2) Rewrite the file:line resolution order: exact match in file_paths -> exact (repo_root / path_str).is_file() on disk (covers package.json and other non-indexed real files) -> (feat_dir / path_str).is_file() (covers self-cites like spec.md:15; line-count check against that doc) -> basename match restricted to feature_files (unique -> resolve; multiple -> verify line-count against each, all pass = verified, else 'ambiguous') -> basename match against all file_paths (unique -> resolve; multiple -> status 'ambiguous' with reason 'basename matches N files - qualify the citation'). Only return 'contradicted' when zero candidates exist anywhere AND nothing on disk. Never index into an unordered set. (3) calls/uses branch: when subject or object bare-name is not in symbol_names, return 'ambiguous' with reason 'not a repo symbol (possibly stdlib/third-party)' instead of 'contradicted'; only 'contradicted' may remain for cases with positive evidence (none currently exist for this branch, so effectively missing-symbol always softens to ambiguous). Since only contradicted triggers demote, ambiguous results stop the false demotions while still surfacing in the MD report's Ambiguous section. DESIGN DECISIONS for implementer: (i) whether has_method missing-symbol (lines 228-236) also softens to ambiguous - recommend yes for missing method, keep contradicted only when the class exists but method doesn't and the file is fully indexed; (ii) whether multi-candidate basenames that ALL satisfy the line count count as verified (recommend yes, deterministic). Must also update test_contradicted_call_flagged which asserts the old policy.
defects.tests_to_add: In tests/context/domains/test_reality_check.py (extend the fake_context fixture with two same-basename files, one owned by the feature): (1) bare `__init__.py:98` where the feature-owned __init__.py has 200 lines and a sibling has 0 -> verified, never resolved to the empty sibling; (2) basename matching 3 non-feature files -> status ambiguous, not contradicted; (3) `package.json:2` present on disk but absent from map/files.json -> verified; (4) `spec.md:3` self-cite resolving against the feature dir -> verified; (5) `X` uses `os.environ.setdefault` with X in symbols -> ambiguous, and demote_feature_on_contradiction leaves confidence untouched; (6) regression: a genuinely-over-the-end line cite on a fully-qualified path stays contradicted; (7) CLI: --demote run with only ambiguous findings exits 0 and does not rewrite feature.json.

defects.symptom: reality-check --demote is one-way: after fixing the docs and re-running clean, confidence stays AMBIGUOUS forever. Sessions hand-edited feature.json + INDEX.json back to EXTRACTED/INFERRED because no CLI verb restores confidence ('mark-enriched only clears the pending marker; there's no explicit confidence setter, and reality-check only demotes, never promotes'). Covers the no-promote half of evidence item 8 and the hand-edit-confidence workarounds in item 5.

defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED. demote_feature_on_contradiction (reality_check.py:464-498) writes ConfidenceLevel.AMBIGUOUS into feature.json and mirrors it into INDEX.json, discarding the previous confidence. Grep across dummyindex/ shows no promote/confidence-set code path anywhere; mark-enriched's domain op only unlinks a marker file. Reproduced: --demote flipped a temp feature EXTRACTED->AMBIGUOUS in both JSON files; no command restores it.
defects.proposed_fix: Make demotion reversible and symmetric, no new top-level verb needed. (1) In demote_feature_on_contradiction, before overwriting, record the prior value: payload['confidence_demoted_from'] = <previous confidence> (only when not already AMBIGUOUS). (2) Add promote_feature_on_clean(features_dir, report) -> bool in domains/reality_check.py: when report.contradicted == 0, feature.json confidence == AMBIGUOUS and 'confidence_demoted_from' is present, restore confidence from it, drop the marker key, and mirror into INDEX.json (same atomic-write idiom as demote, lines 485-498). (3) In cli/reality_check.py run(), when --demote is passed and the report is contradiction-free, call promote_feature_on_clean - so the documented loop 'fix docs, re-run reality-check --demote' self-heals. DESIGN DECISION: auto-restore on a clean --demote run (recommended - symmetric, zero new CLI surface) vs an explicit --promote flag; if reviewers prefer explicitness, add --promote as a bare flag alongside --demote in the same arg loop (cli/reality_check.py:22-28). Note: features demoted by older versions lack confidence_demoted_from - promote should then be a no-op (documented), or optionally fall back to EXTRACTED only under an explicit flag (leave as no-op by default). Update dummyindex/skills/council/45-reality-check.md to describe the restore behaviour.
defects.tests_to_add: test_demote_records_prior_confidence (feature.json gains confidence_demoted_from=EXTRACTED); test_clean_rerun_restores_confidence (demote -> fix doc -> re-run --demote -> confidence back to EXTRACTED in feature.json AND INDEX.json, marker key removed); test_promote_noop_without_demotion_record (hand-set AMBIGUOUS without the marker stays AMBIGUOUS); test_promote_idempotent (second clean run no-op); CLI test mirroring test_cli_reality_check_subcommand asserting exit 0 + restored index entry.

defects.symptom: council-batch cannot drive the documented scoped re-council: (a) `--feature <id>` is SILENTLY swallowed (exit 0, whole-repo frontier - reproduced at HEAD), so 'scoped' runs are an illusion; (b) the frontier is council-log-completeness-only, so drifted-but-already-enriched features (complete logs, stale content) are never resurfaced - and there is no --force, no recouncil verb, and no council-log reset; (c) the .hash staleness scheme the resume doc promises ('aggregate source hash ... every stage re-runs', 'write features/<id>/.hash after stage 3') has zero CLI implementation, so sessions recomputed hashes in raw python and hand-dispatched 24 agents; (d) trees whose enrichment predates logging discipline have no recognized stage-2/3 completions, so advancing the loop wants to re-run the architect over ALL features and clobber curated plan.md files - sessions aborted the loop mid-pipeline; (e) reconcile --json drifted_features and the council-batch frontier are disjoint signals with no documented arbitration, causing a misdiagnosed 'corrupted hash cache' and a user-repeated command. Covers evidence items 5, 7, 9, 10, 15, 16 and the .hash half of item 6.

defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED. The CLI loads all INDEX.json feature ids (cli/council_batch.py:73) and next_batch's frontier consults only _council-log.json completeness (domains/council_batch.py:88-94 via council.py:153-163). There is no scoping parameter, no force parameter, and no verb in ContextSubcommand (context/enums.py:32-75) to reset or backfill a council log. The .hash baseline scheme exists only in skill markdown (19-resume.md) - no domain code reads or writes it - so drift can never influence the frontier. Reproduced: `council-batch --next --feature feat-a` exited 0 and returned the unscoped frontier with no warning. NOTE on item 15's framing: the 'pre-0.24 logs not recognized / not migrated' theory is misdiagnosed - stage numbering and agent names in the council-log convention are unchanged since commit 55f7fc9 (stage 1=dev, 2=architect, 3=critic, verified via git show of 20-specify/30-plan/40-critique) - those sessions' logs were simply never written; the confirmed current-code gap is that nothing can backfill or override that state.
defects.proposed_fix: Three coordinated, surgical changes. (1) SCOPING: in cli/council_batch.py, collect repeatable `--feature ID` via pull_repeatable_flag (cli/common.py:60) before parse_kv_flags; if given, validate each id against the INDEX.json set (error exit 2 listing unknown ids) and pass the filtered tuple to next_batch. (2) RE-OPEN: add a reset primitive so the existing frontier logic re-runs a feature without semantics changes - recommended shape: extend the council-log verb (cli/council.py) with a bare `--reset` flag (optionally `--from-stage N`) backed by a new pure function reset_log(features_dir, feature_id, from_stage=1) in domains/council.py that rewrites _council-log.json without entries at stage >= from_stage (atomic tmp+replace, same idiom as append_log lines 124-126); after reset, `council-batch --next --feature <id>` naturally resurfaces the feature at stage 1. DESIGN DECISION: reset-verb (recommended; keeps frontier pure and resumable mid-recouncil) vs a `--force` kwarg on next_batch (rejected: force would pin scoped features at stage 1 forever within a loop since old 'complete' entries persist). The same reset verb is the sanctioned backfill-inverse for pre-logging-era trees: docs must say 'artifact-complete but log-less features can be stamped done via council-log --status complete per stage, or re-run via reset'. (3) DOCS/ARBITRATION: update skills/council/22-parallel-dispatch.md, 19-resume.md and 65-reconcile.md so /dummyindex --recouncil <id> maps to `council-log --reset --feature <id>` + the `council-batch --next --feature <id> ...` loop, and state the arbitration rule explicitly: reconcile --json drifted_features is the staleness authority, the council-batch frontier is only resumption state (resolves item 16). OPTIONAL (separate design decision, larger): move the .hash lifecycle into the CLI (write features/<id>/.hash on stage-3 completion via council-log, surface 'stale' features in council-batch output) - flag for a follow-up rather than blocking this fix.

defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED by repro: `python3 -m dummyindex context section-write --feature feat-a --section security --from-file ...` created features/feat-a/security.md with exit 0. write_section has no canonical allowlist (unlike merge_feature's _VALID_MERGE_SECTIONS), and the v0.14 legacy-essay transition list explicitly includes 'security', so the most likely wrong value is the most readily accepted one.
defects.proposed_fix: Mirror the merge precedent: add _VALID_WRITE_SECTIONS = frozenset({'spec', 'plan', 'concerns'}) to domains/features/constants.py (with the same 'extending is a deliberate spec change' comment pointing at the persona docs); in write_section, after the existing sanitization (ops.py:479-481), reject names outside the set by raising the domain's typed error (FeatureRenameError, or introduce a SectionWriteError(FeatureRenameError) for precision) with a message listing the valid names - CLI already maps it to exit 2 + stderr (cli/features.py:236-240). DESIGN DECISION: whether legacy essay names (README/architecture/implementation/data-model/security/product) remain writable during the v0.14 transition - recommend NO at write time (the transition window is read-side only, see reality_check._CANONICAL_DOCS at reality_check.py:48-56) or gate behind an explicit --legacy bare flag if a migration path is still needed. Also tighten skills/agents/critic-security.md (and 40-critique.md) to state that `--section concerns` is the ONLY valid invocation - no fallback section names. SECONDARY NOTE for the implementer: write_section REPLACES the whole target file (ops.py:490-491) while critic docs say 'append your section' - the read-merge-write burden on concurrent critics is likely why agents reached for sibling files; worth a docstring/doc clarification while in there (behavior change out of scope).

not_confirmed: Item 15's specific mechanism ('pre-0.24 enrichment-stage completions not recognized / not migrated by 0.24.0'): MISDIAGNOSED. git show of commit 55f7fc9 (the spec-kit pipeline that predates the 0.19.x index in that session) shows the identical council-log convention - stage 1=dev, 2=architect, 3=critic-<domain>, same CLI invocations - and the convention is unchanged at HEAD, so there was no schema change for 0.24's council-batch to mis-read. The logs were simply never written by those earlier sessions/agents. The real, code-confirmed gap (frontier trusts only _council-log.json, no backfill/reset path, runaway whole-repo stage-2 batches) is covered in the council-batch defect entry.

Item 6, KeyError 'id' on features/INDEX.json: not a code defect - the schema key is and was 'feature_id' (consumed correctly at cli/council_batch.py:25); the session's ad-hoc probe simply guessed the wrong key. The other half of item 6 (.hash baselines missing for some enriched features) is confirmed as a consequence of the .hash lifecycle living only in skill markdown (19-resume.md) with zero CLI implementation - folded into the council-batch defect.

Item 0 ('llm council' request hand-rolled instead of routed to dummyindex): a 0.15.0-era skill-routing/discovery gap, not a defect in the council/reality-check modules at HEAD; the on-demand dummyindex-audit skill now exists and owns this trigger vocabulary. Belongs to the skill-routing cluster.
Item 1 (subagent missing dummyindex on PATH, bypassed atomic placement): environment/installation cluster (uv-tool PATH inheritance + persona-doc PATH guard), outside C8's modules.
Item 4 (no features-split verb, 7 split flags shipped unapplied): confirmed missing verb but belongs to the features-taxonomy/structural-review cluster, not council/reality-check quality.

Item 12 (plan critique panel dispatched nonexistent 'Code Reviewer' subagent), item 14 (plan flow lacks its own quality gate), item 22 (plan-stage deferrals vs user intent): dummyindex-plan cluster, out of C8 scope.
Item 18 (claimed advisor consultation with no recorded dispatch): orchestrating-model transcript behavior, no code defect to confirm in these modules.
Item 19 (audit persona catalog hardcodes nonexistent subagent_type names): dummyindex-audit cluster, out of C8 scope.

Item 20 (dev-pick recommends generic 'Frontend Developer' instead of equipped specialists): working-as-designed at the dev_pick layer (SubagentType values are deliberately the end-user-global agent names, dev_pick.py:28-40); mapping to equipped project specialists is equip-cluster territory.

Item 21 (council-generated spec.md used a realistic ghp_ placeholder): generated-content style issue in a downstream repo's docs; no rule in the council persona docs currently forbids realistic secret-shaped placeholders - a one-line persona-doc addition is possible but there is no code defect to confirm here.
